# Cage Capture Report — real-machine findings

**Source:** the real cage ledger on `arpits-macbook-local` (`~/.cage`), 36,451 calls
across 2026-02 → 2026-07, analyzed with cage 0.31.1.
**Generated by:** `labs/run_all.py` (re-run any time: `CAGE_REAL_LEDGER=~/.cage python labs/run_all.py`).
**Machine-readable version:** [`CAPTURE_REPORT.json`](CAPTURE_REPORT.json).
**Purpose:** surface concrete, evidence-backed defects to **fix cage**, and specify
the **debug logging cage should add** so these are diagnosable next time.

> This is a snapshot of what cage actually captured on this machine — not a
> synthetic test. The numbers below are your real usage (counts only; the ledger
> holds no prompt bodies).

---

## Executive summary

Cage is capturing **spend** well and **savings** essentially not at all, and its
own health signal is misreporting three of four agents. In one line: *the meter
works, the receipt/attribution engine has no real data, and there's no log trail
to explain why.*

Four things matter most:

1. **Zero real tool-savings receipts.** Of 36,451 calls, there are **3** receipts —
   and all 3 are the `cage demo` seed (`task = fix-handover-bug`). **No real
   savings has ever been captured.** Cage's whole differentiator — "what did each
   tool *save*" — is running on demo data. (`receipts-empty`, high)
2. **Capture-health contradicts the ledger.** `state/cursors.json → _health` marks
   **codex, copilot, and kiro as `captured: false`** while the ledger holds 373
   codex, 60 copilot, and 16 kiro rows. The flag conflates "nothing new on the last
   import run" with "capture is broken" — so `cage doctor` would tell you three
   agents are dead when they aren't. (`health-contradiction`, high)
3. **Kiro capture is empty in practice.** 16 calls, **198 input tokens, 0 output** —
   from a macOS Library log cage found but that carries almost nothing. (`kiro-empty`, high)
4. **No debug trail exists.** There is **no `debug.log`** in `~/.cage/state` —
   `CAGE_DEBUG` has never run — so nothing recorded *why* the above happens.
   (`no-debug-log`, medium)

Plus: `copilot/auto` is UNPRICED (24 of 60 copilot calls), the headline token/$
number is 98% cache reads (misleading without a split), and the human-attention
axis (`gap_ms`) is stamped on ~1% of rows.

---

## What cage captured, per agent (real data)

| agent | calls | tokens_in | tokens_out | cost (repriced) | health flag | note |
|-------|------:|----------:|-----------:|----------------:|:-----------:|------|
| claude-code | 36,002 | 8,178,535,219 | 36,858,377 | $7,025.02 | ✅ captured | 98% of tokens are cache reads |
| codex | 373 | 18,425,552 | 101,814 | $17.94 | ❌ captured:false | but 373 rows exist |
| copilot | 60 | 2,164,279 | 27,155 | $3.59 | ❌ captured:false | 24 calls UNPRICED (`copilot/auto`) |
| kiro | 16 | 198 | 0 | $0.0006 | ❌ captured:false | input-only Library log, ~empty |
| **total** | **36,451** | **8.20 B** | **36,987,346** | **$7,046.55** | | 3 receipts (all demo) |

Cache split (all agents): **98.0% of `tokens_in` are cache reads** — fresh input is
162,031,676 of 8,199,125,248 tokens. The `$7,046` headline is dominated by
cache-read billing, not fresh generation.

Per-agent detail lives in each lab's `snapshot/report.txt` (see [`labs/`](labs/)).

---

## Findings & fixes

Severity is about cage's value proposition, not crashes — nothing here is a crash;
cage is fail-open by design. Each finding pairs a **fix** with the **logging cage
should add** so it's self-diagnosing.

### F1 · Tool-savings receipts are effectively absent  · **high** · `receipts-empty`

**Evidence:** `total_receipts = 3`, all with `task = fix-handover-bug` (the demo
seed); `real_receipts = 0` against 36,451 calls.

**Why it matters:** attribution (`insights attrib` / `matrix` / `roi` / `verdict`)
is the product. With no receipts it has nothing to divide — every view shows the
agent as pure cost (net **−$7,046**), which is not the story cage exists to tell.

**Likely cause:** the receipt-emitting paths aren't firing in the real workflow —
graphify is being run directly rather than through `cage data graphify -- …`, and
the fux `cage_receipt.py` / compressor / response-cache shims aren't wired or aren't
pushing. Because there's no log (F7), we can't tell *which*.

**Cage fix:** (a) make the graphify interceptor the default path `cage setup` wires
(it scaffolds `bin/graphify` — verify it's on PATH ahead of the real one), and (b)
have `cage doctor` add a **"receipts: 0 captured — attribution has no data"** check
so the absence is loud, the same way UNPRICED is loud for pricing.

**Logging to add:** in the receipt push path (`graphifymeter.py`, `metering.record_receipt`,
`responsecache.py`, `compress.py`) log at every emit/skip: `tool`, whether a
receipt was produced, and if skipped, *why* (no `source_file` parsed / interceptor
not invoked / push sink unresolved). Today a skipped receipt is silent.

### F2 · Capture-health says `captured:false` while rows exist · **high** · `health-contradiction`

**Evidence:**

| agent | `_health.captured` | rows in ledger | files seen |
|-------|:------------------:|---------------:|-----------:|
| codex | false | 373 | 20 |
| copilot | false | 60 | 152 |
| kiro | false | 16 | 1 |

**Why it matters:** `cage doctor` and the report's capture-health line read this
flag. As written it says three of four agents are capturing nothing — a false
alarm that will send you chasing non-bugs (and mask the real kiro problem, F4,
which looks identical).

**Root cause:** `captured` records "did *this* import run add new rows for the
agent," not "has this agent ever captured." On a run where an agent had nothing new
(or the run only touched claude), the others flip to `false` even with a healthy
history. `_last_import` is 2026-07-19 — the last run simply didn't re-touch them.

**Cage fix:** split the signal into `captured_this_run` **and** `last_captured_ts`
(or `rows_total`), and base the doctor "installed but capturing nothing" ⚠ on
*"files present AND rows_total == 0"*, never on a single run's delta. An agent with
history must not read as broken.

**Logging to add:** in `importcmd` where `_health` is written, log per agent:
`files_seen`, `rows_parsed_this_run`, `rows_new_after_dedupe`, `rows_total`, and the
resolved `src`. That single line would have explained all three `false`s.

### F3 · Kiro capture is empty · **high** · `kiro-empty`

**Evidence:** 16 calls, **198 input tokens, 0 output**, from
`~/Library/Application Support/Kiro/User/globalStorage/kiro.kiroagent/dev_data/tokens_generated.jsonl`
(`files: 1`).

**Why it matters:** kiro is one of the four first-class agents, but its capture is
effectively nothing — and F2 hides this behind the same `captured:false` that codex
and copilot (which *do* have data) also show. Kiro's token log is coarse and
input-only, so even what's captured understates cost (0 output tokens ever).

**Cage fix:** (a) doctor should distinguish "kiro log found but ~0 tokens" from
"codex/copilot healthy," and recommend kiro's proxy fallback (`cage proxy` /
`cage meter -- …`) which is its higher-fidelity path; (b) confirm the Library path
is the right, current Kiro-on-macOS location (it's the VS Code Kiro globalStorage —
verify against a CLI-Kiro install too).

**Logging to add:** log the resolved kiro `src`, whether it existed, byte size, rows
parsed, and tokens summed — so "found but empty" is visible.

### F4 · `copilot/auto` is UNPRICED · **medium** · `unpriced-copilot-auto`

**Evidence:** 24 of 60 copilot calls use model `copilot/auto` → 975,842 tokens
billing **$0**. cage already flags it (`⚠ 24 calls … UNPRICED`).

**Why it matters:** 40% of copilot's captured calls contribute no cost — copilot's
real spend is understated.

**Cage fix / action:** route the router pseudo-model —
`cage prices alias copilot/auto --to copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6` (or whichever model
Copilot's "auto" actually resolves to). Consider shipping a *default* alias for
`copilot/auto` in the bundled policy with a loud "approximate — auto is a router"
note, since every Copilot user hits this.

**Logging to add:** already surfaced in the report; no new logging needed.

### F5 · Headline token/$ is 98% cache reads · **medium** · `cache-dominated-headline`

**Evidence:** cached_in = 8,037,093,572 of tokens_in = 8,199,125,248 (**98.0%**);
fresh input only 162M.

**Why it matters:** "8.2 billion tokens, $7,046" reads as alarming, but it's almost
entirely prefix-cache re-reads billed at 0.1×. Not a bug — but the default view
doesn't separate cache-read cost from fresh cost, so the number misleads.

**Cage fix:** add a cache-efficiency line/column to `report --usd` — cached vs fresh
tokens and the cache-read share of cost — so the headline is honest at a glance.

**Logging to add:** none; this is a reporting/UX addition.

### F6 · No `debug.log` exists · **medium** · `no-debug-log`

**Evidence:** `~/.cage/state/` contains only `cursors.json` and `limits.json` — no
`debug.log`. `CAGE_DEBUG` has never been set.

**Why it matters:** cage is fail-open (swallows errors on the write/capture path),
and its swallow-sites are audited to log *under `CAGE_DEBUG`* — but if no one turns
it on, every silent skip stays silent. That's exactly why F1–F3 are guesswork.

**Cage fix / proposal (the "more debugging" you asked for):**
- Keep fail-open, but make a **minimal capture breadcrumb always-on** (not gated on
  `CAGE_DEBUG`): one line per import run per agent —
  `agent · files_seen · rows_new · rows_total · src` — appended to a small,
  size-capped `state/capture.log` (counts only, PII-safe, cleanup-allowlisted).
- Gate the *verbose* trail (per-file skip reasons, receipt push/skip, parser
  mismatches) behind `CAGE_DEBUG=1` as today.
- `cage doctor --bundle` already collects debug context; have it include the new
  `capture.log` so a report like this one is one command.

### F7 · `gap_ms` (human-attention axis) barely populated · **low** · `gap-ms-sparse`

**Evidence:** 371 of 36,451 rows carry `gap_ms` (~1%).

**Why it matters:** the passive human-attention axis (turn-gap → attention minutes)
needs `gap_ms`, stamped at import where per-turn timestamps exist. At 1% coverage
the derived-attention view has almost no data.

**Cage fix:** verify `transcript.parse` stamps `gap_ms` for the whole claude
transcript, not just a sliver; log when it's skipped (missing previous-turn ts).

### F8 · Import is stale · **low** · `stale-import`

**Evidence:** `_last_import = 2026-07-19` (report shows "last import 3d ago").

**Why it matters:** capture is pull-based; with nothing scheduled, the ledger drifts
behind reality — and every agent flips to `captured:false` (F2) between runs.

**Action:** a user cron line calling `cage import` (cage installs no scheduler by
design), or `cage data watch` in the foreground.

---

## Concrete debug-logging additions (consolidated)

The single highest-leverage change is a **small always-on capture breadcrumb** plus
richer `CAGE_DEBUG` lines at the capture and receipt sites. Specifics:

| where (file) | at | log (counts only) |
|--------------|----|-------------------|
| `importcmd.py` | end of each agent's import, where `_health` is written | `agent · files_seen · rows_parsed · rows_new_after_dedupe · rows_total · resolved_src` → **always-on** `state/capture.log` |
| `transcript.py` | per file that yields 0 rows | reason: `format-mismatch` / `no-usage-rows` / `all-deduped` (CAGE_DEBUG) |
| `graphifymeter.py`, `metering.record_receipt`, `responsecache.py`, `compress.py` | every receipt emit/skip | `tool · produced?(bool) · skip_reason` (CAGE_DEBUG) |
| `freshness.py` / capture-health | when computing `captured` | record `last_captured_ts` + `rows_total` alongside the run delta |
| `receiptprice.py` | on UNPRICED receipt/call | model, why unpriced (already partly surfaced) |

All of it stays **counts-never-content** and cleanup-allowlisted, consistent with
cage's PII discipline. None of it changes a derived number — it only explains the
capture path.

---

## The labs

Five isolated environments under [`labs/`](labs/), each a real per-agent slice of
the ledger you can poke at without touching the others:

| lab | agents | calls | headline |
|-----|--------|------:|----------|
| [`claude-only`](labs/claude-only) | claude-code | 36,002 | the 98%-cache, $7k bulk |
| [`copilot-only`](labs/copilot-only) | copilot | 60 | 40% UNPRICED (`copilot/auto`) |
| [`kiro-only`](labs/kiro-only) | kiro | 16 | ~empty (198 tokens) |
| [`copilot-kiro`](labs/copilot-kiro) | copilot + kiro | 76 | the two weak agents side by side |
| [`playground`](labs/playground) | — | 0 | scratch ledger; seed by hand |

Each lab has a `snapshot/` (committed cage output) and a `README.md`. The raw
`.cage/ledger/` slices are gitignored — regenerate them any time with
`python labs/run_all.py`.

*Codex was omitted from the singles per your scoping; it's one line in
`LAB_AGENTS` (`labs/run_all.py`) to add.*

---

## Reproduce

```bash
cd cage-lab
CAGE_REAL_LEDGER=~/.cage python labs/run_all.py       # rebuild labs + report from live ledger
CAGE_DEBUG=1 CAGE_REAL_LEDGER=~/.cage python labs/run_all.py   # once cage adds the logging above
```

The runner reads your real ledger read-only, never writes to it, and regenerates
both `CAPTURE_REPORT.json` and the per-lab snapshots.
